{"credits": [{"type": "reporter", "user": {"avatar_url": "https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/20418261?v=4", "events_url": "https://api.github.com/users/Kherrisan/events{/privacy}", "followers_url": "https://api.github.com/users/Kherrisan/followers", "following_url": "https://api.github.com/users/Kherrisan/following{/other_user}", "gists_url": "https://api.github.com/users/Kherrisan/gists{/gist_id}", "gravatar_id": "", "html_url": "https://github.com/Kherrisan", "id": 20418261, "login": "Kherrisan", "node_id": "MDQ6VXNlcjIwNDE4MjYx", "organizations_url": "https://api.github.com/users/Kherrisan/orgs", "received_events_url": "https://api.github.com/users/Kherrisan/received_events", "repos_url": "https://api.github.com/users/Kherrisan/repos", "site_admin": false, "starred_url": "https://api.github.com/users/Kherrisan/starred{/owner}{/repo}", "subscriptions_url": "https://api.github.com/users/Kherrisan/subscriptions", "type": "User", "url": "https://api.github.com/users/Kherrisan", "user_view_type": "public"}}], "cve_id": null, "cvss": {"score": 8.3, "vector_string": "CVSS:3.1/AV:N/AC:L/PR:L/UI:N/S:U/C:H/I:H/A:L"}, "cvss_severities": {"cvss_v3": {"score": 8.3, "vector_string": "CVSS:3.1/AV:N/AC:L/PR:L/UI:N/S:U/C:H/I:H/A:L"}, "cvss_v4": {"score": 0.0, "vector_string": null}}, "cwes": [{"cwe_id": "CWE-863", "name": "Incorrect Authorization"}], "description": "### Summary

The bundled device-pair plugin exposed `/pair` on normal chat command surfaces. In affected releases, authorized non-owner chat senders could issue device-pairing bootstrap codes without having owner, admin, or pairing scope.

This issue does not affect unauthenticated users. The caller must already be allowed to send commands to the agent through a configured chat channel.

### Affected configurations

This affects deployments where the bundled device-pair plugin is enabled and a non-owner sender is authorized to use normal chat commands, such as in a configured Telegram, Discord, or Slack agent.

### Impact

A non-owner authorized sender could create a setup code and use it before expiry to enroll a device with operator/node capabilities. That device would then retain persistent credentials until removed.

### Patched Versions

The first stable patched version is `2026.5.4`.

### Mitigations

Upgrade to `openclaw@2026.5.4` or later. Review paired devices and remove any unexpected entries. In shared chat channels, keep command access limited to users who should be allowed to manage device pairing.", "ghsa_id": "GHSA-xr4f-mjxj-w6w5", "github_reviewed_at": "2026-07-02T16:55:09Z", "html_url": "https://github.com/advisories/GHSA-xr4f-mjxj-w6w5", "identifiers": [{"type": "GHSA", "value": "GHSA-xr4f-mjxj-w6w5"}], "nvd_published_at": null, "published_at": "2026-07-02T16:55:09Z", "references": ["https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/security/advisories/GHSA-xr4f-mjxj-w6w5", "https://github.com/advisories/GHSA-xr4f-mjxj-w6w5"], "repository_advisory_url": "https://api.github.com/repos/openclaw/openclaw/security-advisories/GHSA-xr4f-mjxj-w6w5", "severity": "high", "source_code_location": "https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw", "summary": "OpenClaw: Non-owner chat senders could issue device-pairing bootstrap codes", "type": "reviewed", "updated_at": "2026-07-02T16:55:10Z", "url": "https://api.github.com/advisories/GHSA-xr4f-mjxj-w6w5", "vulnerabilities": [{"first_patched_version": "2026.5.4", "package": {"ecosystem": "npm", "name": "openclaw"}, "vulnerable_functions": [], "vulnerable_version_range": "< 2026.5.4"}], "withdrawn_at": null}

reasons: 資産不一致または判定情報不足

title: OpenClaw: Non-owner chat senders could issue device-pairing bootstrap codes
